---
name: note-writing
description: 'Plan, outline, draft, and refine Japanese note posts. Use for note articles, experience reports, thought pieces, how-to posts, and improving drafts with Markdown elements such as bold, strikethrough, quotes, lists, links, file uploads, code blocks, dividers, and alignment.'
argument-hint: '[topic, target reader, goal, or draft text]'
user-invocable: true
---

# Note Writing

## What this skill does
This skill helps create or improve Japanese articles for note from idea to final draft.
It is designed for short briefs, rough memos, or partially written drafts.

## When to Use
- Write a new note article from a short idea
- Turn bullet points into a readable article
- Improve an existing draft for clarity, flow, and tone
- Convert raw content into note-friendly Markdown structure
- Generate titles, leads, outlines, full drafts, or promo copy

## Inputs
Provide any of the following when available.
- Topic
- Target reader
- Purpose of the article
- Desired tone
- Approximate length
- Existing memo or draft

If important inputs are missing, infer reasonable defaults and state them briefly before writing.

## Procedure
1. Identify the article goal, target reader, and one key takeaway.
2. Create 3 title options with different angles.
3. Write a short lead that tells the reader what they will get.
4. Build a clear outline with H2 and H3 headings. Use [the outline template](./assets/outline-template.md) when the user needs a starting structure.
5. Draft the article in natural Japanese with short paragraphs.
6. Add concrete examples, personal experience, comparisons, or steps where useful.
7. Use Markdown intentionally. See [the Markdown usage reference](./references/markdown-elements.md) for guidance.
8. End with a short summary and, if appropriate, the reader's next action.
9. If asked to revise, improve title strength, reduce redundancy, sharpen logic, and keep a note-like tone.

## Output Requirements
Return one or more of the following depending on the request.
1. 3 title candidates
2. Lead paragraph
3. Outline
4. Full draft
5. Revised draft
6. Short SNS promo copy

## Style Guide
- Write in Japanese
- Lead with the conclusion
- Keep paragraphs short
- Prefer concrete nouns and verbs
- Avoid overclaiming
- Keep the tone warm, clear, and readable
- Do not add long preambles before the actual deliverable
